seo_title: DRS-GUI: Dynamic Region Search for Training-Free GUI Grounding
meta_description: A training-free dynamic region search framework for GUI grounding that improves performance and generalization.
body_html:
DRS-GUI improves performance and generalization by yielding a 14% improvement on ScreenSpot-Pro for general and GUI-specific MLLMs.

Dynamic Region Search Framework Lightweight UI Perceptor with human-like perceptual actions (Focus, Shift, and Scatter) Action Planner based on Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS)

Training-free No need for extensive training data

Dynamic region search Progressively explores the interface to generate region proposals

Experiments and Results 14% improvement on ScreenSpot-Pro for general and GUI-specific MLLMs (Qwen2.5-VL-7B and UGround-V1-7B)

source.title: DRS-GUI: Dynamic Region Search for Training-Free GUI Grounding
source.summary: GUI agents powered by Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have demonstrated impressive capability in understanding and executing user instructions. However, accurately grounding instruction-relevant elements from high-resolution screenshots cluttered with irrelevant UI components remains challenging for existing approaches. Inspired by how humans dynamically adjust their perceptual scope to locate task-related regions on complex screens, we propose DRS-GUI, a training-free dynamic region search framework for GUI grounding that can be seamlessly integrated into existing MLLMs. DRS-GUI introduces a lightweight UI Perceptor that performs three human-like perceptual actions (Focus, Shift, and Scatter) to progressively explore the interface and generate region proposals. To dynamically schedule these actions, we further design an Action Planner based on Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS). A region quality reward is employed to evaluate and select the highly instruction-relevant region, efficiently pruning redundant UI elements. Experiments demonstrate that DRS-GUI yields a 14\% improvement on ScreenSpot-Pro for general and GUI-specific MLLMs (Qwen2.5-VL-7B and UGround-V1-7B), significantly enhancing grounding performance and generalization.
